question: How does the unemployment rate in Hawaii compare to Indiana in 2023?
assertion_detail: Validation was disabled for this profiling run.
final_llm_response:
SELECT
  MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) AS Hawaii_Rate_2023,
  MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) AS Indiana_Rate_2023,
  (MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) - MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END)) AS Rate_Difference,
  ROUND(
    (MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) - MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END))
    / NULLIF(MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END), 0) * 100, 2
  ) AS Percent_Difference,
  CASE
    WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) > MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) THEN 'Hawaii higher'
    WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) < MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) THEN 'Indiana higher'
    ELSE 'Equal'
  END AS Comparison
FROM unemployment_rates_by_state
WHERE State IN ('Hawaii', 'Indiana')
  AND State IS NOT NULL
  AND Rate_2022 IS NOT NULL
  AND Rate_2023 IS NOT NULL
  AND Rate_Change IS NOT NULL
  AND State_Rank IS NOT NULL
HAVING
  MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) IS NOT NULL
  AND MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) IS NOT NULL;
sql_query:
SELECT
  MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) AS Hawaii_Rate_2023,
  MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) AS Indiana_Rate_2023,
  (MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) - MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END)) AS Rate_Difference,
  ROUND(
    (MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) - MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END))
    / NULLIF(MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END), 0) * 100, 2
  ) AS Percent_Difference,
  CASE
    WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) > MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) THEN 'Hawaii higher'
    WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) < MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) THEN 'Indiana higher'
    ELSE 'Equal'
  END AS Comparison
FROM unemployment_rates_by_state
WHERE State IN ('Hawaii', 'Indiana')
  AND State IS NOT NULL
  AND Rate_2022 IS NOT NULL
  AND Rate_2023 IS NOT NULL
  AND Rate_Change IS NOT NULL
  AND State_Rank IS NOT NULL
HAVING
  MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Hawaii' THEN Rate_2023 END) IS NOT NULL
  AND MAX(CASE WHEN State = 'Indiana' THEN Rate_2023 END) IS NOT NULL;

question: Compare the unemployment rate in California and the United States in 2023.
assertion_detail: Validation was disabled for this profiling run.
final_llm_response:
(SELECT ur.State AS Region, ur.Rate_2023 AS Rate2023
 FROM unemployment_rates_by_state ur
 WHERE ur.State = 'California' AND ur.Rate_2023 IS NOT NULL)
UNION ALL
(SELECT 'United States' AS Region,
        SUM(ur.Rate_2023 * up.PopulationCount) / SUM(up.PopulationCount) AS Rate2023
 FROM unemployment_rates_by_state ur
 JOIN us_population up ON LOWER(ur.State) = LOWER(up.State) AND up.CensurYear = (SELECT MAX(CensurYear) FROM us_population)
 WHERE ur.Rate_2023 IS NOT NULL
 HAVING SUM(ur.Rate_2023 * up.PopulationCount) IS NOT NULL AND SUM(up.PopulationCount) > 0);
sql_query:
(SELECT ur.State AS Region, ur.Rate_2023 AS Rate2023
 FROM unemployment_rates_by_state ur
 WHERE ur.State = 'California' AND ur.Rate_2023 IS NOT NULL)
UNION ALL
(SELECT 'United States' AS Region,
        SUM(ur.Rate_2023 * up.PopulationCount) / SUM(up.PopulationCount) AS Rate2023
 FROM unemployment_rates_by_state ur
 JOIN us_population up ON LOWER(ur.State) = LOWER(up.State) AND up.CensurYear = (SELECT MAX(CensurYear) FROM us_population)
 WHERE ur.Rate_2023 IS NOT NULL
 HAVING SUM(ur.Rate_2023 * up.PopulationCount) IS NOT NULL AND SUM(up.PopulationCount) > 0);
